question_text: Two springs are stretched by the same force. Spring A has a spring constant of 100 N/m and Spring B has a spring constant of 50 N/m. Which spring will stretch more?

options.label: C
options.text: Both springs will stretch the same amount

options.label: D
options.text: It is impossible to tell without knowing the force

explanation: Spring B has a smaller spring constant (50 N/m compared to 100 N/m), which means it is less stiff and stretches more easily. Using Hooke's law (F = kx), when the same force is applied to both springs, the spring with the smaller k value must have a larger extension (x). Since k is half as large for Spring B, it will stretch twice as far.
